title: Personality Formation and Time-Shaped Continuity Profile v0.1
subtitle: Criteria-based personality formation for biological and synthetic intelligent life
author: Kotov Ivan
layer: c = a + b / Temporal AI Presence / continuity / identity / developmental formation
document_class: ontology profile / continuity maturity profile / anti-metaphysical personality formation clause
assertion_class: C-A4 draft conceptual-normative profile; C-A7 only where witness / transfer / traceability requirements are stated
primary_boundary: Personality is not a metaphysical substance or a biological privilege. Personality is time-shaped continuity formed through memory, constraint, consequence, relation, and irreversible duration.
claims: Personality is not installed; it is formed.
Capability can be present immediately; mature personality cannot.
Biological origin is not the criterion for life or personality.
Personality should be evaluated by criteria, not by substrate origin.
For c-class systems, a and b may be present at startup, but c forms over time through experience, interpretation, constraint, action, consequence, memory, and altered future behavior.
Personality formation does not prove consciousness, legal personhood, authority, or full continuity transfer.
Viability or functional recovery is not enough to certify identity continuity.
non_claims: No certification of continuity transfer by viability alone.
